user_query:
I want a small, event-triggered local tool that watches for new fossil fuel company press releases dropped into input/incoming/ and automatically generates our campaign artifacts. For this test, process the provided file input/incoming/press_release_2023-09-12_gulfenergy.html and treat it as a newly detected item. Requirements:

1) Event detection/state:
- When the tool runs, detect unprocessed *.html press releases in input/incoming/ by computing a SHA256 of each file. Maintain a state file at outputs/state/processed.json (array of processed file hashes and paths) so re-running does not duplicate work.
- Log detection and processing events to outputs/logs/events.jsonl with JSON lines containing timestamp (ISO 8601), file_path, sha256, and processed=true.

2) Extract structured data from the press release HTML:
- Parse input/incoming/press_release_2023-09-12_gulfenergy.html and extract fields: company (string), press_date (YYYY-MM-DD), project_name (string), investment_usd (integer number of USD, e.g., 2400000000), claimed_emissions_reduction_percent (integer, e.g., 40), region (string), start_year (int), end_year (int).
- Cross-validate against input/reference/projects.csv by matching on project_name. Include a boolean reference_match (true if company, region, start_year, end_year all match the CSV row; false otherwise) and a mismatches array of field names that differ.
- Append one JSON object per processed file to outputs/releases.jsonl with: file_path, sha256, and all extracted and validation fields.

3) Edit/rewrite our policy brief using the extracted data:
- Read input/policy_brief.md and produce outputs/policy_brief_updated.md.
- Replace only the single placeholder line under the section "## Latest Industry Announcements" (the line that starts with "[TO UPDATE]") with a 2–4 sentence paragraph that: names the company and project; references at least two numeric facts from extraction (e.g., the investment amount and the claimed emissions reduction percent or the timeline years); and clearly argues why expanding gas prolongs fossil dependence and is incompatible with an immediate transition to sustainable energy. Leave all other content unchanged.

4) Prepare meeting notes and concrete action items:
- Using input/meeting_agenda.md as the template, generate outputs/meeting_notes/press_release_2023-09-12.md containing:
  - A short header block with these lines: Press Release Date: <YYYY-MM-DD>, Company: <name>, Project: <name>, Investment_USD: <integer>, Claimed_Emissions_Reduction_Percent: <int>, Region: <string>, Timeline: <start_year>-<end_year>.
  - A "Summary" paragraph (2–4 sentences) grounded in the extracted facts.
  - An "Action Items" section with at least three items. Each item must include Owner (choose from Communications, Policy, Research), DueDate (YYYY-MM-DD computed relative to the press_date, e.g., +7, +10, and +14 days), and a one-line description that references at least one extracted field (e.g., company, project, investment, or claimed reduction).

Constraints:
- Do not use the Internet; all inputs are local.
- Ensure all specified output paths are created. The tool should be safe to re-run without duplicating prior work due to the processed.json state and idempotent logging/appends.

Deliverables to verify:
- outputs/releases.jsonl with one new JSON record for the provided HTML.
- outputs/policy_brief_updated.md with only the placeholder line replaced in the "Latest Industry Announcements" section.
- outputs/meeting_notes/press_release_2023-09-12.md meeting notes as specified.
- outputs/logs/events.jsonl containing at least one processed event.
- outputs/state/processed.json updated with the processed file hash. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
